data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture justification, overlay).

A T1 bright-line plausibly fires: the plan proposes a NOVEL mechanism (code-drift detection that invalidates/flags a cryptographic attestation), which is a small architectural pattern with an established body of prior art it does not reference. The plan is a short epic stub, so this is a PLAN-under-specification finding, not a codebase-fact gap.

FINDING (T1(a) — prior art not considered before committing):
- [ ] Before committing to a drift-detection mechanism, name and weigh the established prior art so the approach isn't reinvented from scratch — both (a) the project's OWN already-shipped material-fingerprint binding (the parent `5fd2` attestation binds to the latest material event commit + a head_sha staleness check; see `src/rebar/signing.py` head_sha binding and `5fd2`'s attestation design), which this epic explicitly extends, and (b) the well-trodden external patterns for "has the input this artifact depended on changed": content-addressed / dependency hashing of the resolved edit-set (Bazel/Nix/build-cache staleness), test-impact-analysis / affected-file selection, and cache-invalidation-by-input-digest. State which the plan adopts/repackages and why a new pattern (if any) is justified vs. simply hashing the cited-file/edit-set contents at review time and re-checking on claim.
  - criteria: T1
  - location: plan "Acceptance Criteria" lines 1–2 ("A mechanism detects material drift in the code the review was grounded against (e.g. the resolved edit-set / cited files)"; "Drift invalidates or flags the attestation … cheap enough not to tax hot paths")
  - evidence: ABSENCE — the plan names the goal (detect material code drift → invalidate/flag) and even a cheapness constraint, but cites NO prior art: it does not reference the parent epic's existing head_sha/material-fingerprint binding it builds on, nor any external dependency-hashing / test-impact / cache-invalidation precedent. T1(a) requires prior art be considered before committing.
  - suggested_fix: Add a short "Prior art / approach" note: (1) cross-reference `5fd2`'s material-fingerprint + head_sha staleness binding as the baseline this extends (so this is framed as an extension, not a fresh invention); (2) name the candidate established technique for the new part — e.g. record a content digest of the resolved edit-set / cited files on the attestation at review time and re-compare on the fast claim path (mirrors build-cache input-digest invalidation; keeps it off hot paths per AC2); (3) if a more novel approach is chosen instead, justify it against that baseline (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three).

AFFIRMATIONS (T1):
- T1(c) unverified-capability assertions: the plan makes no "library supports X" / external-capability claims to resolve — it stays within rebar's own attestation model, so there is nothing unverified to ground here.

This is the only T1 finding; the chunk is otherwise clean for this pass.
